zh.Kirkham Priory, Kirkham, Yorkshire, Engl: 英國英格蘭越可爾吉可哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Ros II於大約1255年出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，他的父親Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley27歲，母親Isabel d'Aubigny23歲。他於1287年與Maud de Vaux在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1292年被列為one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i。他去世於1316年8月8日在英國越可爾尤爾頓享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾吉可哈姆。

zh-hans.Newstead, Lincolnshire, England: 英国林可爾史爾紐斯亞德
zh-hans.Kirkham Priory, Kirkham, Yorkshire, Engl: 英国英格兰越可爾吉可哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de Ros II于大约1255年出生在英国越可爾爾姆斯利时，他的父亲Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley27岁，母亲Isabel d'Aubigny23岁。他于1287年与Maud de Vaux在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1292年被列为one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i。他去世于1316年8月8日在英国越可爾尤爾頓享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾吉可哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Ros II was born about 1255, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, was 27 and his mother, Isabel d'Aubigny, was 23. He married Maud de Vaux in 1287, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. In 1292, at the age of 38, his occupation is listed as one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i. He died on 8 August 1316, in Youlton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in Kirkham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Ros II est né environ 1255, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Isabel d'Aubigny, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Maud de Vaux en 1287, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. En 1292, à l’âge de 38 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i. Il est décédé le 8 août 1316, à Youlton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Kirkham, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de Ros II um 1255 in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley 27 und seine Mutter Isabel d'Aubigny 23. Er hat 1287 in Yorkshire, England Maud de Vaux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1292, als er 38 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i aufgeführt. Er starb am 8. August 1316 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Youlton, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Kirkham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Ros II è nato il circa 1255 nel Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Isabel d'Aubigny, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Maud de Vaux nel 1287, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Nel 1292, all'età di 38 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i. Morì il 8 agosto 1316, nel Youlton, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Kirkham, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de Ros IIが約1255年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヘルムスリーで生まれた時，父Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsleyは27歳，母Isabel d'Aubignyは23歳であった。1287年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでMaud de Vauxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。1292年，38歳の時，職業はone of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward iと記録されている。彼は1316年8月8日にイギリスヨークシュアイアイオウルトンで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアキルクハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Ros II(이)가 대략 1255년 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel d'Aubigny(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1287년 영국 요르크실르에서 Maud de Vaux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1292년, 38세의 나이로 그의 직업은 one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i이었다. 그는 1316년 8월 8일 영국 요르크실르 요우턴에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 커카에 묻혔다.

mn.Kirkham Priory, Kirkham, Yorkshire, Engl: Kirkham, Eoferwic, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Ros II nasceu aproximadamente 1255, em Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Isabel d'Aubigny, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud de Vaux em 1287, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Em 1292, com 38 anos, sua profissão aparece como one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i. Ele faleceu em 8 de agosto de 1316, em Youlton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Kirkham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de Ros II родился около 1255 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel d'Aubigny, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Vaux в 1287 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. В 1292 году, в возрасте 38 лет,  он работал по профессии one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i. Он умер 8 августа 1316 г. в местности еултон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности кирхам, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William de Ros II nació aproximadamente 1255, en Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert de Ros III of Helmsley, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Isabel d'Aubigny, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Maud de Vaux en 1287, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. En 1292, a la edad de 38 años, su ocupación registrada era one of the claimants of the crown of scotland during the reign of king edward i. Murió el 8 de agosto de 1316, en Youlton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Kirkham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
